title: Real or Rendered: Spot the Fake
synopsis: You're a junior investigator at a news agency where AI-generated images, voices, and text keep slipping through — and it's your job to catch them before they go public. Every choice you make changes what gets published and who gets hurt.
rationale: As AI-generated content floods social feeds, students this age are primary targets of synthetic misinformation yet receive almost no structured practice in detection before they encounter it.
learning_outcome: Students can apply a repeatable verification checklist to images, audio, and written content to judge whether it was likely AI-generated, and explain their reasoning to someone else.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
Tali Horowitz: The Key Issue with AI Chatbots and Teens
Digital Citizenship: Teaching Teens to Navigate the online World Responsibly | BWWT Ep. 240
nearest_existing: Every New Medium Rewrites What We Believe
